Overall:
The following processes were found to be effective for the removal of glyphosate: chemical treatment with TiO2 (95 percent removal), chlorine (> 99 percent), granular activated carbon, membrane filtration (up to 99 percent), ozone (60 to > 99 percent removal), ultraviolet irradiation with TiO2 (10 to > 99 percent), and ultraviolet irradiation hydrogen peroxide with TiO2 (42 to 60 percent removal).
Studies indicate that the removal achieved with oxidation using chlorine and ozone is important for glyphosate removal.  Permanganate at 1 mg/L for only 15 minutes resulted in 74 percent removal.  The Best Available Technology is GAC adsorption.
Processes ineffective for the removal of glyphosate or not typical to drinking water treatment were chlorine dioxide (20 percent removal), conventional treatment (6 percent), and hydrogen peroxide (no removal).
